critique.episode_title: We Have an Obligation to Help the Poor

episode_source.name: Stand to Reason Weekly Podcast / Podbean

notices.notice_text:
Independent critique of “We Have an Obligation to Help the Poor”

I’ve published an evidence-focused critique of this Stand to Reason episode. It recognizes the episode’s stronger points while examining several claims that may require additional evidence or argument.

Contents:
i. Thesis and overview
ii. Method
iii. Research base
iv. Claim map
v. 1. Jesus’ mission and social justice
vi. 2. Charity, panhandlers, and Christian organizations
vii. 3. Dementia and salvation security
viii. 4. Conscience and the Holy Spirit
ix. 5. Exclusivism and sincere seekers
x. Overall assessment
xi. Evidence needed
xii. AI prompt

Read the critique:
https://onreason.com/episodes/2026-07-15-we-have-an-obligation-to-help-the-poor/

Substantive corrections, objections, and responses are welcome.

history.note: Official Podbean episode page has no public comment control. No matching episode-specific YouTube, Facebook, or X discussion was available; unrelated posts were intentionally avoided.
